g.title: Solar Panel — Feeds charge controller via MC4 connectors
Wanderer 10A PWM Charge Controller — Manages battery charging from solar, provides 12V output
12V 7Ah SLA Battery — Main power source, charged by solar via controller
LM2596 Buck Converter — Steps 12V down to 5V for ESP32. 1000µF cap on output.
1000µF electrolytic capacitor — Smooths 5V output from buck converter
ESP32-WROOM-32U — Main microcontroller. External antenna. Runs irrigation logic, sensor polling, WiFi server.
Waveshare MCP23017 I/O Expander — I2C address 0x27. 16 GPIO pins drive L298N boards 1-4 for valves 1-8. Pull-ups on SDA/SCL.
MOSFET Power Gate — IRF4905 P-channel FET switched by 2N3904 NPN transistor. GPIO 2 controls 12V power to all L298N motor drivers. 1kΩ base resistor, 10kΩ gate pull-up.
L298N Motor Driver Board 1 — Ch A: Valve 1 (Garden drip), Ch B: Valve 2 (Grapes drip). Controlled via MCP23017 PA0-PA3.
L298N Motor Driver Board 2 — Ch A: Valve 3 (Fruit Trees), Ch B: Valve 4 (South Lawn). Controlled via MCP23017 PA4-PA7.
L298N Motor Driver Board 3 — Ch A: Valve 5 (East Lawn), Ch B: Valve 6 (West/NW Lawn). Controlled via MCP23017 PB0-PB3.
L298N Motor Driver Board 4 — Ch A: Valve 7 (NE Lawn), Ch B: Valve 8 (Peonies drip). Controlled via MCP23017 PB4-PB7.
L298N Motor Driver Board 5 — Ch A: Valve 9, Ch B: Valve 10. Controlled DIRECTLY from ESP32 GPIO 25/26/27/14 (no MCP23017).
Valve Box 1 (East Side) — Contains valves 1-4: Garden drip, Grapes drip, Fruit Trees, South Lawn. Fed by L298N boards 1-2.
Valve Box 2 (Southeast Corner) — Contains valves 5-8: East Lawn, West/NW Lawn, NE Lawn, Peonies drip. Fed by L298N boards 3-4.
Zones 9-10 — Direct ESP32 GPIO control via L298N Board 5. Not in a valve box.
DHT22 Temperature & Humidity Sensor — Connected to GPIO 4. Provides ambient readings for irrigation decisions.
Capacitive Soil Moisture Sensors 1-4 — Connected to GPIO 32-35 (ADC). Measure volumetric water content in each zone.
Battery Voltage Divider (6:1) — Scales 12V battery voltage to ~2V for ESP32 ADC on GPIO 36 (SVP). Monitors battery health.
Home Mini-PC — Runs smart-garden-server (Flask) at server (LAN). Schedules irrigation, collects sensor data, serves web UI.
Architecture overview — I2C expander handles valves 1-8, direct GPIO handles valves 9-10

g.text: Z1: Garden drip Z2: Grapes drip Z3: Fruit Trees Z4: South Lawn
Z5: East Lawn Z6: W/NW Lawn Z7: NE Lawn Z8: Peonies drip

svg.text: Smart Garden Wiring Diagram v2 — MCP23017 I/O Expander Architecture — May 2026
ESP32 → I2C → MCP23017 → L298N Boards 1-4 (Valves 1-8) | ESP32 → GPIO direct → L298N Board 5 (Valves 9-10)
